name: Nansen
category: smart-money intelligence / on-chain analytics (entity-labeled wallet + token + smart-money data)
api_type: REST (HTTPS, all endpoints POST with JSON bodies)
auth: API key (header `apikey: YOUR_API_KEY`); key generated in-app at app.nansen.ai/auth/agent-setup. No OAuth. MCP server uses a separate header `NANSEN-API-KEY`.
mcp_detail: First-party hosted remote MCP server at https://mcp.nansen.ai/ra/mcp — streamable HTTP transport (clients often connect via `npx -y mcp-remote` or `claude mcp add --transport http nansen https://mcp.nansen.ai/ra/mcp`); auth via NANSEN-API-KEY header; ~24 tools (smart-money holdings/perp trades, token holders/flows/DEX trades/screener/OHLCV/quant+Nansen scores, address portfolio/transactions/counterparties/related-addresses, wallet PnL, Hyperliquid leaderboard, general_search). Docs: https://docs.nansen.ai/mcp/overview. A community wrapper also exists (patriondigital-nansen-mcp on LobeHub) but is NOT the official one.
pricing_summary: Subscription simplified on 2025-09-25 to two plans: Free ($0/mo; 100 trial API credits + 10 credits/day refill) and Pro ($49/mo billed annually, or $69/mo monthly; includes 2,000 API credits to start, credits expire 1yr from purchase). Old Pioneer (~$99-129/mo) and Professional ($999+/mo) tiers were RETIRED and folded into the single Pro plan. API is credit-metered per call: most foundational endpoints = 1 credit; Smart Money / indicators ≈ 5 credits; up to 150 (Token God Mode), 500 (Profiler premium labels), and 750 credits (Agent Expert Mode). Extra credits purchasable on either plan; exact $-per-credit for top-ups is not public.
data_coverage: 37 networks total: 24 EVM (Ethereum, Arbitrum, Avalanche, Base, Bitlayer, BNB Chain, Celo, Chiliz, Gravity, HyperEVM, IOTA EVM, Linea, Mantle, Monad, Optimism, Plasma, Polygon, Ronin, Scroll, Sei, Sonic, Unichain, Viction, zkSync) + 13 non-EVM (Algorand, Aptos, Bitcoin, Hyperliquid, NEAR, Solana, Stacks, Starknet, Stellar, SUI, TON, Tron)
NOTE: chain support varies by endpoint (Smart Money ~8 chains, Token God Mode ~7, Profiler ~5)
Smart Money: curated top ~5,000 highest-performing wallets by realized profit — net flows, holdings, historical holdings, DEX trades, Jupiter DCAs, Hyperliquid perp trades
Token God Mode: token screener/discovery, holder analysis (with labels), token flows (hourly by segment), DEX trades, transfers, who-bought/sold, OHLCV, quant/Nansen scores, perp positions
Profiler (wallet): current + historical balances, transactions, wallet PnL summary & per-token PnL, counterparties, related addresses (first funders/signers/deployed contracts), labels
Entity labels: human-readable labels on hundreds of millions of addresses (exchanges, funds, market makers, notable individuals)
Additional: Agent (natural-language query, fast/expert modes), Prediction Markets (Polymarket categories/markets/orderbooks/trades/PnL), Portfolio/DeFi positions, Backtesting (historical data)
signal_strengths: Best-in-class smart-money tracking: curated ~5k top-performing wallets with net inflow/outflow per token — directly surfaces accumulation/distribution signals
Proprietary entity labeling at massive scale (exchanges, funds, market makers, named investors) — turns raw addresses into actionable who-is-doing-what intelligence
Token God Mode flow intelligence segmented by holder type (smart money vs exchanges vs retail) for early token rotation/momentum signals
Wallet Profiler with realized PnL per wallet/token — identify and follow consistently profitable traders
Cross-chain breadth incl. Solana, Bitcoin, Hyperliquid perps and Polymarket prediction-market signals
First-party hosted MCP + 'Agent' NL endpoint make it unusually agent/LLM-ready for autonomous trading-signal pipelines
weaknesses: Credit-metered API can get expensive at scale; premium calls (Profiler premium labels 500cr, Agent Expert 750cr) burn the monthly allowance fast and exact $-per-extra-credit is not published
Chain coverage is uneven — full 37-chain list does not apply to every endpoint (Smart Money/Profiler cover far fewer)
Smart-money universe is a curated/opaque set (top ~5k wallets) — selection methodology not fully transparent
MCP server runs over plain HTTP (clients need --allow-http flag), a minor security/config wrinkle
No free unlimited tier for production API use; Free plan's 10 credits/day refill is only enough for light testing
Labels/PnL are Nansen-proprietary — vendor lock-in and no easy way to independently audit label accuracy

name: Surf (AskSurf.ai) — Surf API / Surf Skill (developer data API for AI agents)
category: Aggregated crypto data API + analyst-LLM layer for AI agents (smart-money labels, on-chain SQL, market/social/prediction-market data). NOT a single raw-data source — it is a unifier that 'replaces 60+ APIs'.
api_type: REST (OpenAPI 3 spec published); includes a raw on-chain SQL endpoint over ClickHouse
auth: API key as Bearer token (Authorization: Bearer $SURF_API_KEY). 30 free credits/day anonymously with NO key; full access requires signup at agents.asksurf.ai and pay-as-you-go credits. Separate billing from the consumer Chat/Studio subscription.
mcp_detail: Official: github.com/asksurf-ai/surf-mcp (MIT) → npm @surf-ai/surf-mcp. Transport: stdio, launched via `npx -y @surf-ai/surf-mcp` (or bunx), env SURF_API_KEY. Dynamically generates 12 grouped tools covering 86 endpoints from api.asksurf.ai/gateway/openapi.json via @surf-ai/sdk (HTTP under the hood). Tools: surf_market, surf_exchange, surf_wallet, surf_token, surf_social, surf_project, surf_onchain, surf_search, surf_prediction_market, surf_fund, surf_news, surf_web. Low adoption (~6 stars, 2 forks; last commit Apr 3 2026). Also official: surf-skills (agent Skill, 71 stars, v0.0.6 Jun 12 2026, `npx skills add asksurf-ai/surf-skills`) and surf-cli (Go, the binary the Skill drives).
pricing_summary: Developer/API side: 30 free credits/day with no key; then pay-as-you-go credits (buy after signup at agents.asksurf.ai) — credit-metered, each response returns meta.credits_used; per-call credit cost is not published as a public table. Launch promo: $200K in free API credits given away, tiered $3–~$100 per claimant. Consumer side (asksurf.ai Chat/Studio): two paid tiers Pro and Max (Max has 7 sub-tiers); Studio iterations/data-calls/DB-ops consume monthly-refreshing credits. Exact USD/month for Pro & Max are NOT public (pricing page is JS-rendered and did not expose figures). The 'Plus' tier was discontinued 2026-04-01. API key billing is separate from the Chat/Studio subscription.
data_coverage: 83+ commands across ~15 data domains (12 grouped tool families via MCP / 86 endpoints)

Market: real-time prices from 16 CEX (Binance, OKX, Bybit, Bitget, Coinbase...), OHLCV candles, pre-computed technical indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger, EMA, SMA), rankings, Fear & Greed, liquidations, futures/options, ETF flows
Exchange/derivatives: order books, funding rates, long/short ratios, perpetuals; dedicated Hyperliquid domain (positions, fills, traders)
Wallet: balances, transfers, DeFi positions, net worth, and named wallet LABELS — maps 100M+ wallets across 13 networks (exchanges, whales, funds, protocols, bridges, MEV bots)

On-chain SQL: direct SQL access to 80+ analyst-ready ClickHouse tables (DEX trades, transfers, prices, TVL, lending, staking, bridges) across 7+ chains, sub-second queries; plus tx lookup, gas prices, structured-query
Token: holders, DEX trades, transfers, unlock schedules, tokenomics
Social: X/Twitter profiles, posts, followers, sentiment, mindshare, smart-follower metrics
Prediction markets: Polymarket & Kalshi (markets, trades, prices, volume, open interest/odds)
Project/DeFi: protocol profiles, TVL, DeFi rankings; Fund: VC fund profiles/portfolios/rankings
News & Search: crypto news from 30+ sources auto-classified by AI (category/sentiment/relevance) + Surf-original research; cross-domain entity search; surf_web fetches arbitrary URLs as markdown
Aggregates 200+ data sources across 40+ blockchains; explicitly markets itself as replacing 60+ independent APIs
signal_strengths: Smart-money / entity labeling: named identification of 100M+ wallets (whales, funds, CEX, MEV bots) across 13 networks — strong for following-the-money trade signals
Unifies normally-siloed signal sources (CEX+DEX prices, on-chain, social mindshare, prediction-market odds, derivatives/Hyperliquid) behind one credit-metered key and one OpenAPI spec
Raw on-chain SQL over 80+ pre-modeled ClickHouse tables lets agents compute custom signals (flows, accumulation, bridge activity) without running their own indexer
Pre-computed technical indicators + 16-CEX aggregated prices reduce client-side compute for market signals
Built specifically for LLM/agent consumption: natural-language→endpoint mapping, structured JSON envelope with credits_used, official MCP + Skill so Claude/Cursor/Windsurf call it with zero glue code
AI-classified news + Surf-original research adds an analyst/LLM layer on top of raw data (the part that differs from a pure data provider)
weaknesses: Aggregator, not the source of truth: it wraps third-party providers, so latency, accuracy and coverage inherit upstream limits, and underlying vendors are not individually named/auditable
Pricing opacity: consumer Pro/Max USD tiers and per-call credit costs are not published in machine-readable form (marketing pages are JS-rendered); hard to model unit economics in advance
Credit-metered everything (including raw SQL and web fetch) can make heavy/exploratory signal workloads costly vs. a flat-rate raw provider
Official MCP repo is early-stage and lightly maintained (~6 stars, last commit Apr 3 2026) and only stdio — no documented hosted/remote MCP endpoint
On-chain SQL limited to ~7 chains despite '40+ blockchains' marketing for higher-level endpoints; depth varies by chain
Newer entrant (2026), small GitHub traction; durability/SLA for production agents unproven
Some headline numbers are inconsistent across pages (83+ vs 86 vs 90+ endpoints, 13 vs 40+ chains) — marketing rounding, treat with caution

name: Dune Analytics — Query / Execution API
category: querying-platform (custom SQL over decoded on-chain data + community dashboards)
api_type: REST. api.dune.com/api/v1: POST /query/{query_id}/execute -> execution_id; GET /execution/{execution_id}/status; GET /execution/{execution_id}/results (JSON) & /results/csv; GET /query/{query_id}/results (latest cached); POST /execution/{execution_id}/cancel. Supports filters on cached results + dynamic query params. Plus Trino connector (SQL warehouse) and dbt connector.
auth: API key (X-Dune-API-Key header). Included on all plans, including Free (subject to credit spend limit).
mcp_detail: OFFICIAL hosted Dune MCP: Remote MCP over Streamable HTTP at https://api.dune.com/mcp/v1 (POST /mcp/v1 calls, GET /mcp/v1 stream resume). Auth: OAuth 2.0 + PKCE (S256, scope mcp:dune:full) for browser agents, or API key via x-dune-api-key header / ?api_key=. Exposes 29 tools in 6 categories: Discovery (search docs, find tables by protocol/chain, list chains, contract lookup), Query Lifecycle (create/fetch/update/execute + results), Materialized Views (build/schedule/refresh), Visualization (charts/counters/tables), Dashboard (create/update/archive), Account (credit usage) + 2 resources (DuneSQL perf, function refs). Docs: docs.dune.com/api-reference/agents/mcp. Setup via `claude mcp add`, Cursor/Claude Desktop config, OpenCode/Codex CLI. SEPARATELY, several COMMUNITY (unofficial) MCP servers exist over the REST API: kukapay/dune-analytics-mcp (Python, most popular), ekailabs/dune-mcp-server (TS/Bun), demomagic/dune-mcp-server, deacix/dune-mcp — stdio, not maintained by Dune.
pricing_summary: Credit-based, 4 tiers: Free ($0, 2,500 credits/mo), Analyst, Plus, Enterprise (custom). Older naming 'Plus ~$399/mo' and 'Premium ~$999/mo' appears in 2024-25 sources (POSSIBLY STALE — current docs/pricing use Free/Analyst/Plus/Enterprise; exact base $ + included credits for Analyst/Plus are NOT clearly published as of 2026-06). Overage per 100 credits: Free $5.00; Analyst $1.875 monthly / $1.625 annual; Plus $1.596 monthly / $1.396 annual; Enterprise custom. API export conversion: Free & Analyst = 1 credit / 1,000 datapoints; Plus = 1 credit / 5,000 datapoints (datapoint = rows*columns, or bytes/100, whichever greater). Rate limits: Free ~15 rpm low + 40 rpm high (~55 combined); Plus 70 + 200 (~270); Enterprise 350+/1000+ rpm; per-IP 1000 rps all plans.
data_coverage: Custom SQL (DuneSQL / Trino dialect) over decoded on-chain data across 100+ blockchains
Decoded contract events & calls; raw + abstraction tables (dex.trades, nft.trades, prices, etc.)
Query execution + cached results (persist 90 days), CSV + JSON export
Thousands of reusable community queries & dashboards
Materialized views / scheduled refreshes
Warehouse/BI access via Trino (Hex, Metabase, DBeaver) and dbt write pipelines
signal_strengths: Best-in-class for ARBITRARY custom SQL over decoded multi-chain on-chain data — any metric expressible in SQL
Massive library of community queries/dashboards (DEX volume, TVL, protocol revenue, wallet flows) reusable as building blocks
Execution API parametrizes & re-runs any saved query programmatically (execute -> poll status -> fetch results/CSV)
Official SDKs (Python dune-client, TS @duneanalytics/client, Go duneapi-client-go) auto-handle auth/polling/pagination
Enterprise warehouse access via Trino + dbt connectors for production pipelines
Official hosted MCP makes the full query/dashboard lifecycle agent-callable with OAuth or API key
weaknesses: NOT realtime — query queue + run + poll latency; analytics-oriented, not low-latency trading signals (use Sim APIs for realtime)
Credit costs scale with datapoints returned; large exports get expensive on lower tiers
Requires SQL knowledge + familiarity with Dune table schema/abstractions
Exact Analyst/Plus base prices & included credit allotments not transparently published (only Free=2,500 + overage rates are concrete)
Free-tier rate limits (~55 rpm) and credit cap are low for production

name: Dune Sim APIs (sim.dune.com)
category: raw EVM/SVM data — realtime multichain wallet/transaction data (DeFi/market data adjacent)
api_type: REST. EVM: /v1/evm/balances/{address}, /evm/activity, /evm/transactions, /evm/token-info, /evm/token-holders, /evm/collectibles, /evm/subscriptions (webhooks). SVM (beta): /beta/svm/balances, /beta/svm/transactions (param chains=solana|eclipse|all). JSON responses, HTTPS only.
auth: API key via X-Sim-Api-Key header; HTTPS-only (plain HTTP fails). Keys generated in Sim dashboard at sim.dune.com. Single key works across all supported chains.
mcp_detail: No dedicated official Sim-specific MCP server documented; the official Dune MCP (api.dune.com/mcp/v1) targets the Query/Dashboard API, not Sim's realtime endpoints. No clearly official Sim MCP found as of 2026-06.
pricing_summary: Usage-based (Compute Units / CUs), scales free -> enterprise. Free/developer tier reported at up to ~1,000,000 CUs and 5 RPS for development; billed by monthly CU volume (e.g. Balances ~1 CU per chain per request); 'no hidden fees'. NOTE: sim.dune.com/pricing now 308-redirects to dune.com/pricing (Sim pricing folded into unified Dune pricing) — exact paid CU bundles/$ tiers NOT clearly public as of 2026-06; the 1M CU / 5 RPS free figure is from secondary/search sources, treat as approximate / POSSIBLY STALE.
data_coverage: Realtime EVM data across 60+ chains (Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, Optimism, etc.)
Realtime SVM data (Solana + Eclipse) — currently BETA
Token balances (native + ERC20; SPL + SPL-2022 on SVM) with enriched metadata + USD prices
Wallet activity feed (native/ERC20/NFT transfers, chronological)
Granular transactions (block info, gas, tx type)
Token info/metadata + realtime USD price; token holders ranked by balance; NFT collectibles (ERC721/ERC1155)
Webhook subscriptions (push on balance changes / activity / transactions)
signal_strengths: Realtime, indexer-free reads — built for low-latency wallet/portfolio/activity signals (complements Dune's batch SQL)
Single API key covers 60+ EVM chains plus Solana/Eclipse — strong multichain breadth
Balances enriched with USD pricing out of the box (live portfolio valuation)
Webhook/subscription push model for realtime balance-change & activity triggers (event-driven signals)
Token-holders + activity endpoints useful for near-realtime smart-money / wallet-flow tracking
weaknesses: SVM/Solana endpoints in BETA (/beta/svm/...) with different params (chains vs chain_ids); only balances + transactions so far
Not arbitrary-SQL — fixed endpoint set; custom metrics require falling back to the Dune Query API
Exact paid pricing/CU bundles not transparently published (pricing page redirects to unified Dune pricing); free-tier CU/RPS from secondary sources
5 RPS free-tier cap is low for high-throughput realtime use
Pricing/branding consolidation with Dune in flux — standalone Sim pricing references may be stale

name: Arkham Intelligence
category: smart-money intelligence / entity & wallet de-anonymization (address attribution + fund-flow tracking)
api_type: REST + WebSocket (real-time transfer streaming)
auth: API key (sent in 'API-Key' header). Access granted via an application/request form at intel.arkm.com/api; no self-serve signup visible.
mcp_detail: No official Arkham MCP server. Community options only: (1) Vyntral/arkham-intelligence-claude-skill on GitHub (a Claude skill wrapping the API, not a true MCP server); (2) Apify actors 'Arkham Intelligence Wallet Data Scraper' and 'Arkham AI Transaction Analyzer' (scrapers exposed via Apify's API/MCP, not official); (3) Unofficial Arkham API reverse-engineering repos (cipher-rc5.github.io/UnofficialArkhamAPI). None are first-party.
pricing_summary: Not public. Operates on a freemium model for the web platform (free tier + paid Pro/Enterprise per third-party reviews), but the developer API has no published price list or tier names — pricing/rate-limit tiers are quoted after an access request at intel.arkm.com/api. WebSocket usage is metered (1 API credit per transfer; caps of 10,000 transfers/hour, 1,000,000/month). The 'new' Arkham API was announced Feb 17, 2026 (recent).
data_coverage: Entity attribution: addresses linked to real-world entities (exchanges, funds, whales, protocols, DAOs, individuals) via the 'Ultra' address-matching engine
Endpoints: /intelligence/address, /intelligence/entity, /intelligence/entity_predictions (ML-predicted addresses), /intelligence/contract (deployer), /labels, /tag, /cluster (wallet clustering)
Fund-flow & transactions: /transfers, /swaps (DEX), /tx/{hash}, /counterparties (who an address/entity trades with), token top-flow & volume
Portfolio & history: /portfolio (entity/address, point-in-time + daily time-series), /history (value over time)
Token analytics: top/trending tokens, top holders by CoinGecko ID or contract address
Multi-chain: Ethereum, Bitcoin, Solana, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, Optimism, Avalanche, BNB Smart Chain and more (full list via /chains)
User-defined custom entities (create/update address groupings)

Separate product (not the API): Arkham Intel Exchange — an ARKM-token marketplace for buying/selling wallet labels & intel via bounties/auctions (2.5% maker / 5% taker fee; 90-day exclusive access window)
signal_strengths: Best-in-class entity/identity attribution — de-anonymizes wallets to named real-world entities (its core differentiator vs pure raw-data providers)
Whale & smart-money tracking: counterparty graphs, entity portfolios and historical holdings, ML entity-prediction to expand wallet clusters
Real-time whale-movement alerting via WebSocket transfer stream
Deep fund-flow tracing across exchanges, funds and protocols (multi-chain), useful for following money in/out of CEXs
Token holder concentration and DEX swap flow for early/whale-positioning signals
weaknesses: Pricing and API tiers are not transparent — must request access, hard to budget
No official MCP server; agent integration requires building your own wrapper or relying on unofficial/scraper tooling
Attribution can include probabilistic/ML 'predictions' (entity_predictions) that may be wrong — labels are not all human-verified
Heavy endpoints (/transfers, /swaps, /counterparties, top_flow, volume) are throttled to 1 req/sec, limiting bulk historical pulls
API is relatively new (rebuilt Feb 2026) — endpoint surface may still be changing
Macro/statistical on-chain indicators (SOPR, MVRV, etc.) are NOT its focus — it is entity/flow-centric, not a market-indicator engine
docs_url: https://intel.arkm.com/api (docs at arkm.com/api/docs; mock at docs.intel.arkm.com)

name: Glassnode
category: macro on-chain & market-data analytics (statistical on-chain indicators / Studio API)
api_type: REST (JSON). Base URL https://api.glassnode.com/v1/ . Also CLI tool and Excel add-in.
auth: API key, passed either as 'X-Api-Key' header or 'api_key' query parameter. Key generated at studio.glassnode.com/settings/api. Requests consume data credits tied to subscription tier.
mcp_detail: Official Glassnode MCP server at https://mcp.glassnode.com (HTTP transport, typically via the mcp-remote wrapper). Currently in BETA with temporary free public access (no key needed, but public access is capped to 30-day time ranges); with an X-Api-Key it consumes API credits like the REST API. Exposes ~6-7 tools: get_assets_list, get_metrics_list, get_asset_metrics, get_metric_metadata, fetch_metric, fetch_bulk_metrics. Supports 1,700+ assets and 900+ metrics. Docs: docs.glassnode.com/integrations-and-tools/glassnode-mcp-server
pricing_summary: Per live studio.glassnode.com/pricing (2026): ADVANCED $49/mo billed annually — 40+ market + 270+ on-chain metrics, 24h resolution, 4yr history, personal use; full REST API NOT included (only separate 'API Light': 14-day history, daily resolution, 50 calls/day). PROFESSIONAL = custom pricing — 180+ market + 570+ on-chain metrics, up to 10-min resolution, 15+ yr history, commercial license; full Professional API is a paid add-on with credit-based usage (50→unlimited calls/mo) incl. bulk/point-in-time/entity-adjusted/per-exchange endpoints. GLASSNODE VECTOR from $749/mo (separate cycle-navigation product). ENTERPRISE/Data Shares custom. NOTE: some third-party reviews still cite a free 'Standard' tier and a ~$26.10/mo Advanced price — that appears stale vs the current pricing page; flag as possibly outdated.
data_coverage: Indicators: SOPR (and aSOPR, SOPR by age cohort), MVRV (incl. entity-adjusted MVRV, MVRV-Z score), NUPL, NVT, Reserve Risk, Realized Cap, dormancy/lifespan
Market metrics: price, market cap, realized price, futures/options/derivatives data
Exchange flows: exchange in/out flows, exchange balances, net position change, per-exchange breakdowns (Professional)
Addresses & entities: active/new/sending/receiving addresses, addresses by balance band, entity-adjusted metrics (dedupes intra-entity transfers)
Supply distribution: HODL waves, supply held by cohorts (LTH/STH, whales, miners), unspent supply, UTXO age
Mining: miner balances, hash rate, block stats; Breakdowns endpoints for cohort decomposition
Coverage: 1,700+ assets/tokens and 900+ metrics; resolutions from 10-minute (Professional) up to daily; history up to 15+ years
signal_strengths: Gold-standard macro on-chain indicators: SOPR, MVRV / MVRV-Z, NUPL, Reserve Risk for cycle-top/bottom and profit-taking regime signals
Entity-adjusted metrics that strip intra-entity noise for a cleaner economic signal (a Glassnode differentiator)
Exchange-flow and exchange-balance data for supply-shock / sell-pressure signals, with per-exchange granularity on Professional
Cohort/HODL-wave and address-balance-band breakdowns (LTH vs STH, whale accumulation) for distribution-phase signals
Deep history (15+ yr) and fine resolution (10-min) on Professional for backtesting indicator strategies
First-party, beta-free MCP server makes it directly usable by LLM trading/analytics agents
weaknesses: Real API access is expensive/gated: Advanced ($49/mo) only ships the crippled 'API Light' (14-day history, daily res, 50 calls/day); meaningful programmatic use needs Professional, which is custom-priced (effectively four figures historically)
Aggregate/statistical metrics, NOT wallet-level identity — it will not tell you WHO a wallet is (that's Arkham's lane)
Credit-based metering on top of subscription can make heavy/bulk pulls costly
MCP server is beta ('tools subject to change') and public (keyless) access is capped to 30-day ranges, limiting agent-driven historical analysis without a paid key
Tier gating: best signals (SOPR variants, entity-adjusted, per-exchange, point-in-time) sit in higher tiers; lower tiers see reduced metric set and resolution
Some publicly indexed pricing is inconsistent/possibly stale (free Standard tier and $26.10 Advanced no longer on the live page)

name: Allium
category: Enterprise on-chain data warehouse / indexed blockchain data platform (raw + decoded + analytics)
api_type: mixed (REST Realtime APIs + SQL via Explorer/data-warehouse + streaming: Kafka, GCP Pub/Sub, AWS SNS, WebSocket)
auth: API key (X-API-KEY header; keys generated at app.allium.so/settings/api-keys). Also supports machine/agent payments (x402 EIP-712 USDC-on-Base, Tempo MPP) to pay-per-call without signup.
mcp_detail: https://mcp.allium.so — official, maintained by Allium Labs (installer package @allium-labs/mcp). HTTP transport, API-key auth via X-API-KEY header. Tools: explorer_run_query (saved queries), explorer_run_sql (raw SQL), explorer_fetch_schema, explorer_search_schemas, plus Realtime API tools. Marketed as 'AgentHub' (agents.allium.so). Installer supports 13+ clients (Claude Code, Cursor, Claude Desktop, VS Code/Copilot, Codex, Cline, Windsurf, Warp, Gemini CLI, Goose, Zed, Opencode) plus manual setup for ChatGPT/Claude/Copilot/Gemini and LangGraph/LangChain. Docs: docs.allium.so/ai/mcp/overview.
pricing_summary: Not public for enterprise — quote-based, contact sales. Web sources indicate enterprise contracts start in the high four figures/month (~$5K+/mo) and scale with event volume; streaming costs more than batch warehouse drops (SLA/throughput). Developer tier: free API key with limited daily usage at app.allium.so for evaluation; production via account upgrade OR per-call machine payments (USDC on Base via x402 / Tempo MPP, no plan commitment).
data_coverage: 50-80+ chains for full data warehouse (decoded), 20+ chains for low-latency Realtime API, marketing cites up to 150+ chains for normalized data via MCP/AgentHub
Raw tables: blocks, transactions, logs, traces
Decoded events per protocol
Token tables: ERC-20/721/1155 transfers, token prices (current/historical/volume), metadata
DEX tables, wallet labels/entity tags, cross-chain transfers
Realtime: prices, balances/holdings, P&L, tx history, DeFi positions (LP/lending/staking/spot/perps) at 3-5s freshness, ~50-100ms response, P50 3s latency, 99.9% uptime, 100K+ RPS
Delivery: Datashares into customer's own Snowflake / BigQuery / Databricks; Datastreams (Kafka/PubSub/SNS/WS); Beam custom filtered pipelines
signal_strengths: Enterprise data-warehouse model: decoded tables delivered directly into the customer's own Snowflake/BigQuery/Databricks — best for heavy custom SQL analytics and joining on-chain with internal data
Curated wallet labels / entity tagging (exchanges, protocols, funds) enables smart-money / entity-attribution signals
Cross-chain transfer + DEX tables normalized across 50+ chains in one schema
Low-latency Realtime REST (3-5s freshness, 100K+ RPS) for production apps and balances/P&L/DeFi-position signals
Official MCP + agent-native machine payments (x402/Tempo) positions it well for autonomous trading/analytics agents
weaknesses: Pricing opaque and enterprise-gated (high four-figures+/month); not hobbyist-friendly beyond the eval free key
Full decoded warehouse coverage (50+ chains) is broader than the 20+ chain low-latency Realtime API — real-time chain set is narrower
Chain-count figures vary across pages (50+/80+/150+), suggesting marketing inconsistency
Warehouse delivery (Datashares) requires you to bring your own Snowflake/BigQuery/Databricks — added cost/complexity

name: Bitquery
category: Indexed multi-chain blockchain data API (GraphQL) + real-time streaming; strong DEX/trading data
api_type: mixed — primary unified GraphQL (V1 40+ chains, V2); plus real-time streaming via WebSocket (GraphQL subscriptions), Kafka, and Solana/EVM gRPC (CoreCast); SQL/ClickHouse access and bulk cloud datashares (Snowflake/BigQuery/S3/Azure)
auth: API key (OAuth-style access token / Bearer) for GraphQL & streams; MCP uses OAuth 2.1 browser sign-in or query-token. Free tier requires signup; Commercial via sales.
mcp_detail: https://mcp.bitquery.io — official, production-grade, backed by Bitquery's proprietary trading dataset. Connect via `npx -y mcp-remote https://mcp.bitquery.io/mcp` (mcp-remote utility; standard MCP/JSON-RPC, no manual stdio/SSE config). Auth: OAuth 2.1 with browser sign-in (caches ~30-day refresh token) OR query-token (?token=ory_...) for autonomous agents. Tools (small surface): per-trade swap rows (price/USD/trader wallet), pre-built OHLC candles (1s–daily), token economics (mkt cap/FDV/supply), outlier-filtered volume — currently across ~8 chains (trading dataset). Clients: Claude Desktop/Web, ChatGPT, Cursor, Claude Code, VS Code, any MCP client. Note: also exposed as a community-listed toolkit on Composio.
pricing_summary: Points-based, usage-driven (revised model rolled out June 2024). Official pricing page (bitquery.io/pricing) currently lists only: Developer = FREE ($0/mo, 1,000 points trial, 10 req/min, 2 concurrent streams, 10 rows/request); Commercial = custom 'talk to our team' (unlimited API calls/no throttling, custom points & streams, SLA, SQL/Cloud/Kafka, dedicated support); Datashares & Exports = custom. Realtime queries charged 5 points per 'cube'. Streams are sold separately as N concurrent streams (no row/rate limits) and do NOT consume points on paid plans. POSSIBLY STALE: third-party/older sources (G2, ~2024) cite fixed tiers Team $249 / Startup $449 / Growth $999 / Business $1999 — these are NOT on the current official page and appear deprecated; treat fixed dollar tiers as stale.
data_coverage: 40+ blockchains via one unified GraphQL schema (V1 docs cite 40+; EVM + non-EVM incl. Solana, Tron, etc.)
DEX: trades/swaps across 300+ DEX protocols, 1.8M+ token pairs (Uniswap V2/V3/V4, PancakeSwap, Curve, Raydium, Jupiter, Aerodrome, etc.)
OHLCV candles at any interval; USD token prices derived from on-chain swaps
Liquidity/pools (reserves, TVL, LP events), new-pair detection
Wallet analytics: top traders by volume/PnL/trade count
Mempool / pending transactions streaming (EVM)
Raw transfers, transactions, balances, smart-contract events; historical + real-time on every dataset
Bulk datashares on Snowflake/BigQuery/S3/Azure
signal_strengths: Best-in-class real-time DEX trade & OHLCV streaming: WebSocket ~1s, Kafka <500ms, gRPC/CoreCast <300ms — built for trading bots, scanners, new-pair/liquidity alerting
New-pair-the-moment-it's-created detection + per-trade swap rows with trader wallet = strong alpha/sniping & smart-money signals
Single unified GraphQL schema across 40+ chains lowers integration cost vs per-chain indexers
Mempool/pending-tx streaming for front-run/MEV-aware signals
Official OAuth-based MCP exposes trading-grade OHLC + token economics directly to agents
weaknesses: Paid pricing not transparent on official page (Commercial = contact sales); points model can be hard to forecast/budget
Free Developer tier is very limited (1k points trial, 10 req/min, 10 rows/request, 2 streams) — evaluation-only
Public third-party pricing figures are stale/inconsistent with the current usage-based model
MCP trading dataset currently limited to ~8 chains (narrower than the 40+ GraphQL coverage)
Heavy real-time streaming requires the Commercial plan; concurrent streams billed separately

name: The Graph
category: Decentralized indexing protocol — custom subgraphs (GraphQL) + Token API (REST) + Substreams streaming; querying platform
api_type: mixed — GraphQL (custom Subgraphs via Gateway) + REST (Token API, OpenAPI spec, powered by Pinax) + Substreams (gRPC firehose streaming into custom sinks); newer 'Amp' database
auth: Gateway API key from Subgraph Studio for subgraph GraphQL queries; JWT/Bearer access token (thegraph.market) for Token API REST. Billing on Arbitrum (GRT) or credit card.
mcp_detail: TWO official MCP servers (open-source, implement Anthropic MCP), announced by The Graph: (1) Subgraph MCP — endpoint https://subgraphs.mcp.thegraph.com/sse (SSE transport), auth via Gateway API key from Subgraph Studio; tools fetch subgraph schema + execute GraphQL across the network (15,000+/thousands of subgraphs). (2) Token API MCP — endpoint https://token-api.mcp.thegraph.com/ , auth via thegraph.market JWT ACCESS_TOKEN. Clients: Claude Desktop, Cline, Cursor, ChatGPT. SEPARATE community server also exists: kukapay/thegraph-mcp (MIT, NOT official; tools getSubgraphSchema/querySubgraph, THEGRAPH_API_KEY) and PaulieB14/subgraph-mcp-skills. Docs: thegraph.com/docs/en/ai-suite/subgraph-mcp/ and /token-api-mcp/.
pricing_summary: Subgraph queries: Free Plan = 100,000 queries/month free; beyond that $2 per 100,000 queries (usage-based; e.g. 300K queries = $4/mo). Pay with credit card or crypto (GRT on Arbitrum; needs ETH on Arbitrum for gas). Unlimited subgraph creation/testing included. Token API and Substreams pricing not detailed on the Studio pricing page (some Token API endpoints marked FREE, e.g. health/version/network lookups; advanced features plan-gated). Q4'25 trend: Substreams revenue up ~4x QoQ while traditional subgraph query fees down ~8.7% QoQ.
data_coverage: 60+ networks/chains for indexing (Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Solana, Bitcoin, Base, Optimism, Avalanche, and more)
Custom Subgraphs: any smart-contract event/entity the developer defines (fully custom indexing) — 15K+ deployed subgraphs, 75K+ projects, 1.27T+ queries served, 100+ indexer nodes
Token API (REST, via Pinax): token balances, transfers, holders, NFT data, DEX info, OHLC/prices across ~5 primary chains (Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Solana, Tron) + 23+ listed
Substreams: high-throughput streaming/firehose into custom sinks (gRPC)
Amp: blockchain-first database (newer product)
signal_strengths: Custom subgraph indexing is uniquely flexible — define exactly the protocol/event entities you need (no other vendor lets you author arbitrary indexed schemas) for bespoke DeFi/NFT/DAO/gaming analytics
Massive ecosystem of 15K+ existing subgraphs covers most major protocols out-of-the-box (queryable via one Gateway)
Substreams gRPC firehose for high-throughput real-time pipelines into custom sinks (good for backfills + live signal generation)
Token API gives standardized balances/transfers/holders/OHLC without writing a subgraph
Transparent, cheap usage pricing ($2/100K queries) and dual official MCP servers (Subgraph + Token API) make it agent- and developer-friendly
weaknesses: Subgraphs require development effort to author/deploy — not turnkey for raw 'give me all DEX trades' without a matching subgraph; quality/coverage varies by community-authored subgraph
Real-time DEX-trade streaming is weaker/less turnkey than Bitquery; subgraphs have indexing lag and are query-pull not low-latency push (Substreams fills streaming but needs setup)
Token API real-time chain set (~5 primary) is much narrower than the 60+ indexing networks
Decentralized billing in GRT on Arbitrum (gas in ETH) adds friction vs pure SaaS (credit card option mitigates)
Token API/Substreams pricing not clearly published; ecosystem fee streams (Horizon, multiple data services) add complexity
Two official MCPs plus several community ones can confuse which to adopt

name: Covalent / GoldRush (goldrush.dev)
category: unified multi-chain raw + DeFi/market on-chain data API (balances, transactions, tokens, NFTs, DEX, pricing) with AI-agent tooling
api_type: REST (also JSON-RPC passthrough + WebSocket Streaming API in beta; first-party TS/JS, Python, Rust SDKs)
auth: API key — Bearer token (Authorization: Bearer KEY); also Basic-auth username or ?key= query param. Plus keyless x402 pay-per-request (agent pays per call, no account/key).
mcp_detail: Official: github.com/covalenthq/goldrush-mcp-server, npm @covalenthq/goldrush-mcp-server. TypeScript, built on @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + @covalenthq/client-sdk. Transports: stdio (default) + HTTP. Exposes 26 tools (marketing says 27+) across balances/transactions/NFTs/pricing/DEX/cross-chain/Bitcoin. STALE/RISK FLAG: repo ARCHIVED 2026-02-24, last release v0.0.4 on 2025-07-30, only 14 stars — appears superseded by the newer GoldRush Agent Skills approach.
pricing_summary: Free Trial: $0, 25,000 credits, 4 RPS, 14 days. Vibe Coding: $10/mo, 10,000 credits/mo, 4 RPS, flex credits $0.00077/credit. Professional: $250/mo, 300,000 credits/mo, 50 RPS, auto-scaling flex credits. Inner Circle (Enterprise): custom (SLA/NDA/dedicated). Also resold on Google Cloud Marketplace & QuickNode from ~$50/mo. Credit cost varies per endpoint. x402: pay-per-request in stablecoins on Base (testnet live, mainnet pending as of May 2026) — fixed price for balances/NFTs, tiered S/M/L/XL for variable-length data; concrete per-call USD not public.
data_coverage: 100+ chains (EVM + non-EVM): Ethereum, Base, Polygon, BNB Chain, Arbitrum, Optimism, Avalanche, plus Solana and Bitcoin
Full archival history from genesis block on every supported chain
Wallet token balances (ERC-20/721/1155 + native), spot & historical token prices
Full decoded transaction history, event logs, traces
NFT metadata + spam-collection flagging
DEX analytics (XY=K pools: volume, liquidity, swap counts, fees, LP token prices), OHLCV pricing
Security: token approvals / allowances
Token holders, gas prices, multichain balances/transactions/activity aggregation
Streaming API (public beta): sub-second token balances, new Uniswap pairs, wallet activity, OHLCV
signal_strengths: Clean, typed JSON normalized to one schema across 100+ chains — no raw hex/ABI decoding needed, ideal for LLM consumption
Full genesis-to-now archival data, strong for historical/backtesting and wallet-history signals

First-class AI-agent stack: official MCP server, GoldRush Agent Skills (npx skills add covalenthq/goldrush-agent-skills) that document endpoints/params/credit-costs, and x402 keyless per-request payment for autonomous agents
WebSocket Streaming API for reactive agents (new DEX pairs, prices, on-chain events)
Broad non-EVM coverage including Solana and Bitcoin under one API; 99.99% uptime SLA, SOC2, 70k+ devs claimed
DEX/DeFi pool analytics + NFT spam detection useful for trading/risk signals
weaknesses: Official MCP server repo is ARCHIVED (Feb 2026) with very low stars and a mid-2025 last release — likely deprecated in favor of Agent Skills; integration path is in flux
x402 micropayment rail is mainnet-pending (only Base Sepolia testnet live as of late May 2026) — not production-ready for paid agent traffic yet
Credit-based metering with per-endpoint variable costs makes cost forecasting harder; heavy/complex endpoints burn more credits
Free trial is time-boxed (14 days / 25k credits) rather than a perpetual free tier
Positioned increasingly around 'AI data availability/verifiability' narrative; some marketing tool counts (27+) differ slightly from actual repo (26)
sources: gh repo view covalenthq/goldrush-mcp-server (verified: isArchived=true, pushedAt 2026-02-20, 14 stars, MIT)

name: Moralis (moralis.io / moralis.com)
category: unified multi-chain Web3 Data API (balances, transactions, tokens, NFTs, DeFi, prices) + AI-native data layer (Cortex/MCP)
api_type: REST (Web3 Data API, EVM + Solana; also Streams webhooks and RPC nodes). Official JS/Python SDKs.
auth: API key — X-API-Key request header (base URL https://deep-index.moralis.io/api/v2.2). MCP server reads MORALIS_API_KEY env var.
mcp_detail: Official: npm @moralisweb3/api-mcp-server (run via `npx @moralisweb3/api-mcp-server`, needs MORALIS_API_KEY); repo github.com/moralisweb3/moralis-mcp-server (referenced by docs/npm; gh lookup did not resolve the exact slug — npm package is the authoritative official artifact). TypeScript wrapper over the Moralis REST API; local or cloud-deployable; integrations documented for Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf. Part of 'Moralis Cortex' AI-native data layer. NOTE: a separate hosted Cortex REST chat endpoint (https://cortex-api.moralis.io/chat) is being RETIRED 2026-06-04 with users migrated to the MCP/Onchain Skills path. Many third-party community MCP repos exist (a6b8/moralis-mcp etc.) — do not confuse with official.
pricing_summary: Free/Starter: $0, 40,000 Compute Units (CU) per day, 40 RPS, 2 RPC nodes, attribution/branding required. Starter: $49/mo (billed annually), 2,000,000 CU/mo, 40 RPS, $11.25 per extra 1M CU, attribution required. Pro: $199/mo (billed annually), 100,000,000 CU/mo, 80 RPS, 20 RPC nodes, $5 per extra 1M CU. Business: $490/mo (billed annually), 500,000,000 CU/mo, 200 RPS, unlimited RPC nodes, $4 per extra 1M CU, NO attribution required. Enterprise: custom (SLAs, 24/7 eng, committed-use discounts). (Monthly/non-annual prices are higher; some legacy pages cite Pro $249 / Business $999.)
data_coverage: 20+ EVM chains: Ethereum, Polygon, BNB Chain, Avalanche, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, Linea, zkSync, Polygon zkEVM, Blast, Mantle, Cronos, Fantom, Gnosis, Moonbeam, Ronin, PulseChain, opBNB, Zetachain, Chiliz, etc.
Solana (non-EVM) support across Data API
Wallet token/native balances and full transaction & transfer history
NFT data: metadata, ownership, collections, transfers, prices
Token API: prices (spot/historical), metadata, holders, pair/DEX data
DeFi positions / wallet analytics
Token & wallet PnL / profitability and analytics endpoints
Streams (real-time webhook event monitoring) and RPC nodes
signal_strengths: Mature, widely-adopted REST Web3 Data API with deep EVM coverage and strong DeFi/token-price/NFT endpoints (good for trading and wallet-intelligence signals)
Token & wallet PnL/profitability + DeFi position endpoints are useful out-of-the-box trading analytics
AI-native layer (Cortex) lets LLMs query on-chain data in natural language; official MCP server + documented ElizaOS plugin for agent frameworks
Streams product gives real-time push (webhook) event monitoring to drive reactive signals
Simple X-API-Key auth, well-documented OpenAPI, official JS/Python SDKs; generous-feeling daily free CU allotment (40k/day)
weaknesses: AI layer is in transition: hosted Cortex chat API scheduled for REMOVAL 2026-06-04; integration story is migrating to MCP / 'Onchain Skills' — moving target
Official MCP GitHub slug did not resolve via gh during verification (npm package confirms official, but repo discoverability/state is unclear) — possible churn
No Bitcoin / UTXO chain coverage (EVM + Solana focused), unlike GoldRush
Free and lower paid tiers require Moralis attribution/branding (only removed at Business $490/mo+)
CU-based metering with per-endpoint cost weighting; pricing pages vary between annual vs monthly numbers (e.g., Pro shown as $199 annual vs $249 elsewhere), so verify on checkout
No keyless/agent-native micropayment option comparable to GoldRush x402

name: DeFiLlama
category: DeFi/market data (TVL, yields, stablecoins, fees/revenue) — aggregated protocol intelligence
api_type: REST
auth: none for free API; API key for Pro (key embedded in URL path)
mcp_detail: No official DefiLlama MCP. Multiple third-party servers: dcSpark/mcp-server-defillama (npx @mcp-dockmaster/mcp-server-defillama, stdio, ~9 stars, low maturity), IQAIcom/defillama-mcp, nic0xflamel/defillama-mcp (OpenAPI proxy). All wrap the free public REST API.
pricing_summary: Free public API (api.llama.fi): no key, 31 endpoint groups, generous but unspecified rate limit. Pro API (pro-api.llama.fi/{API_KEY}): $300/mo, 1,000 req/min, 1M calls/month, overage $0.60 per 1,000 calls, adds 38 exclusive endpoints (token unlocks, liquidity, ETFs, bridges, RWA, treasuries, equities, advanced yields). Note: a separate $40.83/mo consumer 'Pro Analytics' dashboard subscription exists but does NOT include API access.
data_coverage: TVL (per-protocol and per-chain, historical)
Token prices current + historical by contract address (coins API)
Stablecoins circulating supply and market cap
Yields / APY pool data
DEX volumes
Perpetuals / open interest
Fees and revenue analytics
Pro-only: token unlocks/emissions, fundraising, treasuries, hacks, bridges, ETFs, RWA, equities
signal_strengths: Best-in-class TVL and yield/APY coverage across hundreds of chains and protocols
Stablecoin flows and supply tracking (de-peg / liquidity-rotation signals)
Fees/revenue rankings as protocol-health and rotation signals
Fully free for core TVL/yield/stablecoin/fee data with no key — ideal for low-cost pipelines
Token unlock schedules (Pro) for supply-overhang / sell-pressure signals
weaknesses: Not real-time/tick-level — aggregated and somewhat lagged; weaker for intraday price signals
No official MCP server (only low-star community wrappers)
Pro tier is a steep jump ($300/mo) for unlock/bridge/RWA endpoints
Free rate limit not publicly quantified; subject to throttling
docs.llama.fi/pro-api page returns 403/404 to bots — must use api-docs.defillama.com or the DefiLlama/docs GitHub repo

name: DexScreener
category: Raw DEX data (pairs, prices, liquidity, new/boosted tokens) — multi-chain DEX terminal
api_type: REST
auth: none (keyless, no signup required)
mcp_detail: No official MCP. Community servers: janswist/mcp-dexscreener, openSVM/dexscreener-mcp-server (also on Glama), plus Apify-hosted scraper MCPs. All wrap the free public REST API (stdio for the GitHub ones).
pricing_summary: Free, no API key. Rate limits stated in docs: 60 requests/minute for token-profiles, token-boosts, orders, ads, community-takeovers, and metas/trending endpoints; 300 requests/minute for the DEX pairs/search/tokens/token-pairs endpoints. No public paid API tier (paid offerings are for token-profile/boost listings, not API access).
data_coverage: DEX pairs by chainId/pairId with live price, liquidity, volume, txns
Search across pairs (/latest/dex/search)
Tokens and token-pairs lookup by chain + token address
Latest token profiles and recent updates (new launches)
Token boosts (latest / top) and paid orders/listings
Trending metas (/metas/trending/v1)
signal_strengths: Strongest for NEW PAIR / new-token discovery and freshly listed/boosted tokens (memecoin/early-launch signals)
Live per-pair liquidity, volume, and price across many chains and DEXs in one keyless API
Boosts/profiles/trending surface social and promotional momentum signals
300 rpm on core pair/price endpoints is generous for a free, no-signup API
Zero onboarding friction — ideal for low-cost real-time DEX price/liquidity feeds
weaknesses: No official MCP server (community-only)
No API key means no per-user higher tier — hard ceiling at the stated rate limits
Docs are terse: auth/pricing not stated on reference page (confirmed keyless via API terms + multiple sources)
No historical OHLCV depth comparable to GeckoTerminal/Birdeye; primarily current/near-real-time snapshots
Some third-party 'DexScreener API' offerings are scrapers (Apify/ScrapingBee), not the official endpoint — easy to conflate

name: GeckoTerminal
category: Raw on-chain DEX data (pools, OHLCV, trending/new pools) — DEX-native market terminal
api_type: REST
auth: none for the free public API (no key); same data also served via CoinGecko API /onchain endpoints with a CoinGecko key
mcp_detail: Surfaced through CoinGecko's OFFICIAL MCP (mcp.api.coingecko.com/mcp) — the /onchain (GeckoTerminal) DEX data is exposed there. No standalone GeckoTerminal-branded MCP; treat the CoinGecko MCP as the official channel.
pricing_summary: Free public API, no API key, currently in Beta. Public rate limit ~30 calls/minute. Higher limits require subscribing to a paid CoinGecko API plan (the same on-chain data is available via CoinGecko's /onchain endpoints), which lifts on-chain access to ~250 calls/min on paid tiers.
data_coverage: 200+ networks and 1,500+ DEXs
Pools: trending pools, new pools, top pools per network/dex
Token price, market data, metadata for 1M+ on-chain tokens (8M+ via CoinGecko /onchain)
OHLCV candlestick data per pool
Recent trades per pool
Search for pools/tokens
signal_strengths: Trending-pools and new-pools endpoints are excellent for early DEX momentum and fresh-launch signals
Pool-level OHLCV + trades give chart/volatility signals without running a node
Very broad network/DEX coverage (200+ chains) in a free, keyless API
Shares CoinGecko's data backbone — consistent token identity and metadata
Free tier enough for prototyping on-chain price/liquidity signals
weaknesses: Free rate limit is low (~30 cpm) — too tight for high-frequency polling
API officially in Beta — schema subject to change
Scaling up effectively requires paying for CoinGecko API (no cheap GeckoTerminal-only paid tier)
No GeckoTerminal-branded official MCP; you go through the CoinGecko MCP
apiguide.geckoterminal.com intro page omits hard rate-limit numbers (sourced from CoinGecko support article)

name: CoinGecko
category: DeFi/market data (aggregated CEX+DEX prices, market cap, OHLC) + on-chain via GeckoTerminal /onchain
api_type: REST
auth: Demo API key for free tier; Pro API key for paid tiers (MCP public endpoint needs no key)
mcp_detail: Official CoinGecko MCP. Hosted free public server at https://mcp.api.coingecko.com/mcp (streamable HTTP) with /sse fallback — no key required. Pro/BYOK server at https://mcp.pro-api.coingecko.com/ (use your key for more tools + higher limits). Also an official npm package @coingecko/coingecko-mcp for local stdio. Exposes prices, market data, OHLC, and on-chain DEX (/onchain GeckoTerminal) tools.
pricing_summary: Demo (free): requires free Demo key, ~30 calls/min stable, 10,000 calls/month (some sources cite up to 100 calls/min). Paid (2026): plans start ~$129/mo (Analyst, 500k calls/mo, 500 calls/min), Lite ~$499/mo, Pro ~$999/mo; Enterprise custom. Overage ~$250 per 500,000 calls. Public MCP server is free with no key (shared rate limits).
data_coverage: Aggregated prices, market cap, volume for 15k+ coins across 1,000+ exchanges
Historical OHLC and market charts
Coin metadata, categories, exchanges, derivatives, NFTs
On-chain DEX price + liquidity for 8M+ tokens across 200+ networks via /onchain (GeckoTerminal)
Global market and dominance data
signal_strengths: Official, keyless, hosted MCP — easiest drop-in for AI agents (Claude, Cursor, etc.) with zero setup
Single source spanning both CEX-aggregated market data AND on-chain DEX data (GeckoTerminal) — broad signal surface
Reliable canonical token identity/metadata across 200+ chains
Strong historical OHLC for backtesting price signals
Free Demo tier plus generous public MCP good for low-cost prototyping
weaknesses: Aggregated/market-level — not tick or mempool granularity; weaker for sub-second or brand-new-launch signals than DexScreener/Birdeye
Free Demo limits are modest (10k calls/mo); meaningful scale jumps to $129/mo+
Public MCP shares rate limits across all users and may throttle under load
Paid tiers are pricey relative to DexScreener/GeckoTerminal-free for similar on-chain data
docs.coingecko.com/docs/mcp-server and /reference/mcp-server return 404 to bots; canonical info via mcp.api.coingecko.com and learn pages

name: Birdeye (Birdeye Data Services, birdeye.so)
category: Raw on-chain + DEX/market data (Solana-first, multi-chain) — token analytics & trading data
api_type: REST + WebSocket
auth: API key (x-api-key header); free Standard key available
mcp_detail: Official Birdeye MCP server (Beta) at https://mcp.birdeye.so/mcp; requires a Birdeye API key. Local stdio option via npx/Node with key passed in headers. Exposes prices, OHLCV, DEX liquidity, trending/new/gainers-losers, token metadata + security info across 10+ networks. NOTE: do not confuse with birdeye.com — an unrelated business-reviews company that also ships an MCP.
pricing_summary: CU (compute-unit) based. Free Standard: 30,000 CU/month, 1 request/sec, limited APIs, no extra CU purchases. Paid: Lite $39/mo (1.5M CU, 15 rps), Starter $99/mo (5M CU, 15 rps), Premium $199/mo (15M CU, 50 rps, WebSocket 500 conns), Business $499/mo (60M CU, 100 rps, WebSocket), up to Business B-50 $2,050/mo (500M CU, 150 rps); Growth AutoScale / Enterprise custom. Overage e.g. $23 per 1M CU (Lite) down to $3.50 (top tiers). 60+ APIs, 8 WebSocket stream types.
data_coverage: Real-time token prices, market cap, volume (15k+ coins / 8M+ on-chain tokens)
On-chain DEX price + liquidity across 10+ networks (Solana-first: SOL, ETH, BSC, Base, etc.)
Historical OHLCV
Trending tokens, new listings, gainers/losers, NFTs
Token metadata, social links, and security/risk info
Wallet/portfolio and trade-level data; WebSocket streams (price, txns, new pairs)
signal_strengths: Deepest Solana on-chain coverage — top choice for Solana memecoin/new-pair and liquidity signals
Real-time WebSocket streams (new pairs, price, large txns) for low-latency signals (paid tiers)
Built-in token security/risk metadata — useful rug/scam-filter signal
Official MCP server for direct agent access
Trending / gainers-losers / new-listing endpoints purpose-built for discovery signals
weaknesses: Free tier is small (30k CU/mo, 1 rps) and limited to a subset of APIs — quickly exhausted
CU model makes cost forecasting harder than flat call quotas
MCP and several features still in Beta
Requires an API key even for the MCP (unlike CoinGecko's keyless public MCP)
Name collision: birdeye.com (review/reputation SaaS) is a different company with its own MCP — high risk of source confusion
docs.birdeye.so/docs/pricing returns 403 to bots; pricing sourced via cached/scraped doc content

name: Cielo Finance
category: smart-money / wallet-intelligence
api_type: REST + WebSocket
auth: API key (obtained via build.cielo.finance portal)
mcp_detail: No official or notable community MCP server found as of 2026-06. Cielo offers an llms.txt index (developer.cielo.finance/llms.txt) for AI-agent consumption of docs/OpenAPI, but that is not an MCP server.
pricing_summary: Credit-based. Free: 5,000 credits/mo, 10 credits/s, access to 1 endpoint only. Builder: $89/mo ($80.10 annual), 100,000 credits/mo, 25 credits/s. Architect: $188/mo ($169.20 annual), 250,000 credits/mo, 75 credits/s. Per-call credit costs vary by endpoint (e.g. Get Tracked Wallets 5 credits, Token PnL 5 credits, Aggregated Token PnL 20 credits). Enterprise/higher tiers not publicly priced.
data_coverage: 28 chains incl. Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, and other major EVM/non-EVM networks
2M+ tracked wallets
Labeled/parsed transactions (swap size, protocol, token)
Wallet PnL (realized + unrealized), token PnL, aggregated stats
Real-time transaction feed (feed-api.cielo.finance/api/v1/feed)
Smart-money / wallet discovery
signal_strengths: Copy-trade and wallet-alert primitives: Add Tracked Wallet endpoint with per-wallet notification config (Telegram bot, Discord channel, min USD threshold, tx types, chains)
Real-time multichain feed of complex/labeled transactions for following smart-money in near-real-time
Cross-chain PnL leaderboards / wallet stats to surface profitable traders to copy
WebSocket stream for low-latency transaction events
weaknesses: Strict credit + endpoint gating on free tier (only 1 endpoint accessible)
No MCP server, so agent integration requires custom REST/WS wiring
Exact per-chain coverage list and some endpoint credit costs require reading individual docs pages
Higher-volume / enterprise pricing not published

name: DeBank (DeBank Cloud API)
category: DeFi / portfolio data
api_type: REST (OpenAPI / Pro API)
auth: API key — register at cloud.debank.com, pass header `AccessKey: <key>`
mcp_detail: Community: demcp/demcp-debank-mcp (maintained by the DeMCP org, NOT by DeBank). Exposes ~8 tools (get_chain_info, get_protocol_info, get_token_info, get_pool_info, get_user_assets, get_user_activities, get_user_authorizations, get_collection_nft_list). Requires a DeBank API key. No official DeBank-built MCP server found.
pricing_summary: Unit-based billing — buy 'units' via the cloud.debank.com dashboard; each endpoint consumes units. Concrete per-unit / per-call price is NOT publicly listed (contact hello.cloud@debank.com). Pro Plan rate limit: up to 100 requests/second (higher on request).
data_coverage: 14+ EVM chains explicitly listed (ETH, BSC, xDai/Gnosis, Polygon, Fantom, OEC, HECO, Avalanche, Optimism, Arbitrum, Celo, Moonriver, Cronos, Boba) plus 'all supported chains' aggregate endpoints
User total balance (cross-chain + per-chain)
Per-protocol positions (portfolio_item_list), near-real-time (refreshed within ~1 min, 12h worst-case)
Token list / token balances, NFT lists
Transaction history / user activities
Token & NFT approval (authorization) auditing
Protocol and token metadata
signal_strengths: Best-in-class cross-protocol DeFi position decoding (LP, lending, staking, farms) normalized into portfolio items
Near-real-time portfolio snapshots good for whale/wallet net-worth tracking
Net-value curves (24h history) for wallet performance signals
Approval/authorization data useful for risk and exposure signals
weaknesses: Pricing opaque — no public per-unit numbers, must contact sales
EVM-focused; not a Solana/non-EVM realtime source
Portfolio data is snapshot/refresh-based, not a push/streaming feed (no native webhooks)
Only a community (DeMCP) MCP server, not official
Several unofficial scraper APIs exist (e.g. LuKks/debank, RapidAPI listings) that are NOT the official Cloud API and may break

name: Helius
category: raw Solana data / RPC + enhanced APIs + webhooks
api_type: REST + JSON-RPC + WebSocket (LaserStream WSS) + gRPC + Webhooks
auth: API key (per-key, set via dashboard; HELIUS_API_KEY for tooling/MCP)
mcp_detail: Official: helius-labs/core-ai (npm: helius-mcp; install `claude mcp add helius npx helius-mcp@latest`). stdio transport, MIT license, actively maintained (helius-cli@2.1.0 dated 2026-06-24). Exposes 10 tools: heliusAccount, heliusWallet, heliusAsset, heliusTransaction, heliusChain, heliusStreaming (webhook CRUD), heliusKnowledge, heliusWrite, heliusCompression, expandResult. Auth via setHeliusApiKey / HELIUS_API_KEY / ~/.helius/config.json.
pricing_summary: Free: $0/mo, 1M credits, 10 req/s (1 sendTx/s), webhooks + LaserStream WSS standard methods, no gRPC/staked. Developer: $49/mo, 10M credits ($5/extra M), 50 req/s, staked connections, transactionSubscribe. Business: $499/mo, 100M credits, 200 req/s, full LaserStream WSS+gRPC. Professional: $999/mo, 200M credits, 500 req/s, data add-ons. Enterprise: custom (1B+ credits). LaserStream gRPC/WSS data add-ons (Professional): $500/mo (5 TB) up to $6,000/mo (100 TB), overage 2 credits/0.1 MB. 99.99% uptime on paid.
data_coverage: Solana mainnet + devnet (single-chain focus)
Standard Solana JSON-RPC + archival/historical data
Enhanced/parsed transactions (DAS) and human-readable tx history
Digital Asset Standard (DAS) API: tokens, NFTs, compressed NFTs, token holders
Wallet balances/holdings/history
Webhooks (transaction + account notifications)
LaserStream WebSocket + gRPC realtime streaming with failover
signal_strengths: Solana realtime webhooks (account + transaction triggers) — push notifications for on-chain events, ideal for wallet/whale alerting and copy-trade triggers
LaserStream (WSS + gRPC) low-latency streaming with automatic failover for high-frequency Solana signal pipelines
Enhanced/parsed transactions turn raw Solana into labeled swap/transfer data without custom decoding
DAS API for token-holder and compressed-NFT analytics
Official MCP server makes all of the above directly agent-accessible
weaknesses: Solana-only — no EVM or other chains
Credit accounting can get expensive for heavy streaming (data add-ons scale to thousands/mo)
gRPC/staked connections gated behind paid tiers
It is infrastructure/raw-data, not a curated smart-money product (signals must be derived by the consumer)

name: Hyperliquid
category: DeFi / perps market & positions data
api_type: REST (info endpoint, POST/JSON) + WebSocket (also EVM RPC for HyperEVM)
auth: none for public info/market data; signed wallet actions only required for the trading/exchange endpoint (placing orders)
mcp_detail: No official MCP server. Multiple community servers: kukapay/hyperliquid-info-mcp (read-only data/insights), mektigboy/server-hyperliquid, Impa-Ventures/hyperliquid-mcp, edkdev/hyperliquid-mcp (trading interface). These wrap the public info endpoint / Python SDK.
pricing_summary: Free / not metered by API key. Public info & market data has no paid tier and no key. Rate-limited by IP: REST shares an aggregated weight budget of 1200 per minute; info weights vary (clearinghouseState/l2Book/allMids = weight 2, userRole = 60, most others = 20; some history endpoints add weight per 20 items). Per-address action limit: 1 request per 1 USDC cumulatively traded, with a 10,000-request starting buffer.
data_coverage: Perps: user open positions + margin/account summary via clearinghouseState (per-address, by 42-char hex)
Funding: historical, predicted, and per-user funding history
Market data: open interest, mark/oracle/impact prices, L2 order book, mids, candles
Metadata: perp universe, margin tables, leverage, asset contexts (metaAndAssetCtxs)
Multiple perp DEXs (HIP-3 builder-deployed markets), spot markets
User fills, historical orders, TWAP slice fills
signal_strengths: Authoritative perps order/position data straight from the exchange (no key needed) — positions, leverage, margin, liquidation context per wallet
Funding rate (current + predicted) and open-interest signals for perps sentiment/basis strategies
Per-address clearinghouseState lets you track any wallet's open perp positions for copy/whale-tracking
L2 order book + candles + mids for microstructure and execution signals
Fully public/free for read data — low barrier for analytics
weaknesses: Single venue (Hyperliquid only); not multichain wallet intelligence
No native webhooks for info data — polling REST or WS subscriptions only
IP + per-address weight limits require careful request budgeting at scale
Only community MCP servers (no official); trading actions require wallet signing, adding integration complexity
